sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INTEGER, values ARRAY<INTEGER>, created_at DATE);
INSERT INTO test_data VALUES (2, [4, 5, NULL], DATE '2023-01-05');
INSERT INTO test_data VALUES (3, [NULL, NULL, NULL], DATE '2023-01-10');
SELECT id, array_apply(values, x -> x - 1) AS modified_values, created_at - INTERVAL 5 DAY AS reduced_date FROM test_data WHERE created_at < (SELECT MAX(created_at) FROM test_data) - INTERVAL 2 DAY;
SELECT id, array_apply(values, x -> x + 10) AS incremented_values FROM test_data WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_data WHERE values[1] IS NOT NULL);
SELECT COUNT(*), array_apply(values, x -> x * 2) AS doubled_values FROM test_data GROUP BY ARRAY_LENGTH(values) HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
SELECT id, ARRAY[created_at, created_at - INTERVAL 3 DAY] AS date_arr FROM test_data WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM test_data t2 WHERE t2.created_at > test_data.created_at);
SELECT id, array_apply(values, x -> x IS NULL) AS null_check FROM test_data WHERE created_at > DATE '2023-01-05';
